The  following entries describe a few common features of informal speech.

This Section cannot of course offer a complete analysis of spoken language.

‘ What time? ‘ Half past eight.* ‘ At your place? ‘ No, Andy's.* ‘ Maybe call in on Emma  on the way out? * ‘ Not today I can't.* How long you been waiting here, then?

Lovely, the way she talks about her mother.  That car, is it yours?

But note that English does not Topicalised subjects and objects are repeated by pronouns in these structures Lovely, the way she talks about her mother, (topicalised complement) (topicalised verb phrase) What they did, they invited everybody and then forgot the date.

That meeting I thought I was going to scream, (topicalised adverbial) (topicalised indirect object) The people next door, we don't need to send them a card, do we?

Your mother, can I see her if she's in? (topicalised object) (topicalised subject) Your friend Alex, now he speaks German, maybe he can help us.
